name_en: Ministry of Communications and Information Technology
description: Iran's Ministry of Communications and Information Technology (ICT) oversees telecommunications, postal services, and internet infrastructure. The ministry has played a central role in internet censorship and filtering, particularly after the 2009 presidential election. Under [Reza Taghipour](/people/reza-taghipour) (2009-2012), the ministry cut mobile and SMS services before the election and blocked social networks during the Green Movement protests.
editorial_notes: Key ministry for internet filtering and communications control in Iran.
